The South African Police Services (SAPS) have deployed 15 police officers from the Public Order Police (POPs) unit to assist with policing functions during the 2024 Paris Olympics.

The South African Police Services (SAPS) have deployed 15 police officers from the Public Order Police (POPs) unit to assist with policing functions during the 2024 Paris Olympics.

The seven female and eight male officers left for Paris on Wednesday evening ahead of the two-week summer Olympic games.



National Police spokesperson, Athlenda Mathe, says the SAPS is the only SADC law enforcement agency that will take part in this skills sharing opportunity relating to crowd management control capabilities.



"Following a request from the French Government for members from various security officers including SAPS to provide support to its Interior Security in their mission to ensure a safe and secure event, at the cost of the French Government.



"The police officers have been selected from all nine provinces and each boasts more than 10 years' experience in the Public Order Police unit."



National Police Commissioner, General Fannie Masemola, adds the opportunity to deploy members on a global stage is also a chance to share expertise and knowledge to enhance SAPS crowd management capabilities.



"Our Public Order Police officers have been involved in the policing of several world-class events including the BRICS summit and AGOA which took place without incident and disruptions. We are confident that this opportunity will allow members to come back to share and transfer skills and knowledge to other members which will assist us in enhancing the policing of large gatherings and crowds."
